title: Catalyst Center License Utilization Tracking
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:license`. via custom scripted input polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/licenses/device/count and /dna/intent/api/v1/licenses/device/summary` (deploy per docs/guides/catalyst-center.md ┬¦ Custom Scripted Inputs). Default poll interval: 86400s (24h). Volume: ~1 event per license type per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `licenseType` (string), `totalLicenses` (integer), `consumedLicenses` (integer), `availableLicenses` (integer), `expirationDate` (string).
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input (license poller)
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:license" | stats latest(totalLicenses) as total latest(consumedLicenses) as consumed latest(availableLicenses) as available by licenseType | eval utilization_pct=round(consumed*100/total,1) | eval status=case(utilization_pct>90,"Critical",utilization_pct>75,"Warning",1==1,"Healthy") | sort -utilization_pct
description: Tracks Catalyst Center license utilization by type, alerting when license consumption approaches capacity limits.
value: Running out of licenses prevents onboarding new devices. Proactive tracking ensures license procurement happens before capacity is exhausted.
implementation:
Catalyst Center license data requires polling the Intent API.

API endpoint:
ŌĆó `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/licenses/summary` ŌĆö license counts by type
ŌĆó `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/licenses/device/count` ŌĆö per-device license usage

Create a custom scripted input:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_license/bin/collect_licenses.py]
interval = 86400
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:license
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```

The script polls license summary data daily and outputs fields: `licenseType`, `totalLicenses`, `consumedLicenses`, `availableLicenses`, `expirationDate`.
visualization: Table: licenseType, total, consumed, available, utilization_pct, status; gauge or single value per license type; trend of utilization_pct over 90 days if stored in summary or indexed daily snapshots.
knownFalsePositives:
**License utilization naturally fluctuating near the threshold without action required.** License utilization in the 75-90% range is common for well-sized deployments. This range may persist for months without requiring additional licenses. Distinguish by checking the trend ŌĆö is utilization steadily increasing toward 100%, or oscillating within a stable band? Suppress by alerting only on a sustained upward trend (7-day linear regression showing projected breach within 30 days) rather than a static threshold.

**Device decommissioning temporarily reducing license consumption.** When devices are removed from Catalyst Center, license consumption drops temporarily. If devices are being migrated, consumption will increase again when replacement devices are onboarded. Distinguish by correlating with inventory changes ŌĆö check whether `dc(deviceId)` is decreasing. No suppression needed ŌĆö track as a legitimate utilization change.

**License type not applicable to the Catalyst Center deployment.** Some license features (e.g., DNA Advantage, SD-Access) may show 0% utilization if the corresponding features are not deployed. These zero-utilization licenses should not appear in the utilization tracking dashboard. Distinguish by checking whether the license `type` corresponds to features actually deployed. Suppress by filtering `| where license_consumed>0` for active license utilization tracking.

**License synchronization delay between Cisco Smart Account and Catalyst Center.** There may be a delay between purchasing additional licenses in the Cisco Smart Account and those licenses appearing in Catalyst Center's license pool. During this delay, utilization may appear higher than actual. Distinguish by checking the Cisco Smart Account for recent license additions. No SPL suppression ŌĆö wait for the synchronization to complete (typically within 24 hours).

references.title: Cisco Smart Licensing Using Policy ŌĆö Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **license** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:license`.
ŌĆó Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** with Cisco Smart Licensing configured and active.
ŌĆó RBAC: service account with **SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE** or **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE** for license data access.
ŌĆó Understanding of your Catalyst Center licensing model: DNA Essentials, DNA Advantage, DNA Premier tiers, and their respective feature sets.

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
The TA polls the Catalyst Center license API at the configured interval (default 3600 seconds / 1 hour). Each event represents a license type with its current consumption and entitlement.

Key fields:
ŌĆó `licenseName` or `type`: license tier name (e.g., "DNA Advantage", "DNA Essentials").
ŌĆó `consumed` or `license_consumed`: number of licenses currently in use.
ŌĆó `entitled` or `license_entitled`: total number of licenses purchased/available.
ŌĆó `status`: license status (ACTIVE, EXPIRED, IN_COMPLIANCE, OUT_OF_COMPLIANCE).
ŌĆó `expirationDate`: when the license term expires.

Expected volume: ~1 event per license type per poll. Most deployments have 3-10 license types.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:license"
| stats latest(consumed) as used latest(entitled) as total latest(status) as license_status latest(expirationDate) as expiry by licenseName
| eval utilization_pct=round(used/total*100,1)
| eval days_to_expiry=if(isnotnull(expiry), round((strptime(expiry,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400,0), null())
| eval util_status=case(utilization_pct >= 95, "Critical", utilization_pct >= 85, "Warning", utilization_pct >= 75, "Monitor", 1==1, "Normal")
| eval expiry_status=case(days_to_expiry <= 30, "Expiring Soon", days_to_expiry <= 90, "Expiry Warning", isnotnull(days_to_expiry), "OK", 1==1, "No Expiry Data")
| table licenseName, used, total, utilization_pct, util_status, days_to_expiry, expiry_status, license_status
| sort -utilization_pct
```

Understanding this SPL:
ŌĆó **`latest(consumed) as used`**: Takes the most recent consumption count, avoiding double-counting from multiple poll events.
ŌĆó **`utilization_pct`**: License consumption as a percentage of entitlement. The primary metric for capacity planning.
ŌĆó **`days_to_expiry`**: Days until the license term expires. Requires `expirationDate` to be in a parseable format ŌĆö adjust `strptime` format if needed.
ŌĆó **`util_status`**: Traffic-light classification ŌĆö Ōēź95% is critical (immediate procurement needed), Ōēź85% is warning (plan procurement), Ōēź75% is monitor (track trend).
ŌĆó **`expiry_status`**: Separate alerting for license term expiration ŌĆö even low-utilization licenses need renewal before expiry.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
ŌĆó **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > System > Licensing. Compare the consumed/entitled counts and utilization percentages with the Splunk results.
ŌĆó **Smart Account cross-reference:** Log into the Cisco Smart Account portal and verify the license entitlements match the Splunk `total` values.
ŌĆó **Expiry date check:** Verify the `expirationDate` parsing produces correct `days_to_expiry` values.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
ŌĆó **Dashboard:** License utilization gauges (one per license type), expiry countdown tiles, and a 30-day utilization trend timechart showing consumption growth.
ŌĆó **Alert:** Two alert types: (1) Utilization Ōēź95% ŌåÆ immediate procurement action. (2) Days to expiry Ōēż30 ŌåÆ renewal action. Route both to the licensing/procurement team.
ŌĆó **Budget planning:** Use the 30-day utilization trend to project when licenses will be exhausted and generate procurement lead time estimates.

Step 5 ŌĆö Troubleshoot
ŌĆó **Utilization shows 0% for all licenses:** The `license` input may not be enabled or the API credentials lack permission. Check TA input configuration and RBAC.
ŌĆó **`consumed` field not populated:** The field name may differ across Catalyst Center versions. Run `| fieldsummary` on the raw events to identify the correct consumption field.
ŌĆó **License synchronization delay:** There may be a delay between Cisco Smart Account changes and Catalyst Center reflecting them. Wait up to 24 hours for synchronization.
ŌĆó **Utilization fluctuating near threshold:** Device onboarding/decommissioning causes natural license fluctuation. Alert only on sustained upward trends, not transient spikes.
ŌĆó **No license events arriving:** license tracking requires a custom scripted input; verify deployment and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages.
ŌĆó **NULL or zero values in license counts:** the licensing API may return empty fields for license types not provisioned on the cluster; missing values are expected.
ŌĆó **Fewer records than expected:** check the service account role; credential access denied errors indicate insufficient permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We track how many of your Catalyst Center licenses are being used and warn you before you run out, so new devices can always be added.
